exampleSentencesTranslated: Maybe tomorrow I will go to the movies with my friend.
Maybe she will not come to the party tonight.
Maybe I should try this new dish once.
notes_md:
**có khi** = *maybe*, *possibly*. It shows you’re not sure and the chance feels real but not certain.

It usually comes at the beginning of a clause and is followed by a full sentence.  

It can also mean **“sometimes”** in certain contexts, depending on tone and situation.

Close synonym: **có lẽ** (a bit more neutral and common for “maybe”).

content.sentence: Cảm ơn bạn, tôi ___ không biết.

translation.en: Thank you, I don't know sometimes.

exampleSentences: Có khi ngày mai anh sẽ đi xem phim cùng bạn.
Chị ấy có khi sẽ không đến dự tiệc tối nay đâu.
Em có khi nên thử món ăn mới này một lần.

translation.en: I think that sometimes you will come.

content.sentence: Tôi nghĩ rằng ___ bạn sẽ đến.

example.es: A veces viajaré el fin de semana.
example.vi: Có khi tôi sẽ đi du lịch vào cuối tuần.
example.en: Sometimes I will travel on the weekend.

notes:
có khi = maybe, possibly. It shows you’re not sure and the chance feels real but not certain.

It usually comes at the beginning of a clause and is followed by a full sentence.  

It can also mean “sometimes” in certain contexts, depending on tone and situation.

Close synonym: có lẽ (a bit more neutral and common for “maybe”).
